invariant_definition: Vallabha's distinctive doctrine: *brahman* really transforms into the world without itself being modified. A precisely-articulated third option between Advaita's *vivarta* (no real transformation) and ordinary *pariṇāma* (transformation with modification). The world is therefore real (*satya*) and one with *brahman* (no second reality), but *brahman*-essence is not changed by the transformation. The doctrine is the metaphysical core of Śuddhādvaita.

per_school.definition: *Avikṛta-pariṇāma* is the foundational metaphysical posit. *Brahman* (Kṛṣṇa) really emanates the world from itself without being modified — analogous to a lamp emitting light without losing its luminosity. Both *māyā* (in the Advaita sense) and ordinary *pariṇāma* are rejected.

translator_note: 'Transformation-without-modification' is the standard scholarly gloss. The doctrine has no clean Western analogue; perhaps closest is the patristic *processio* of the divine persons in some readings, but this is distant.
